description: A metrics-first, evidence-based content variation. Every post leads with data, statistics, benchmarks, or measurable outcomes. Best for analytical audiences and B2B markets.
target_persona: Data-oriented decision makers, analysts, finance professionals, SaaS/tech buyers, investors

caption_framework.hook: Lead with a surprising statistic or counterintuitive data point
caption_framework.body: Support with 2-3 data points, methodology or source mention, implication for the reader
caption_framework.cta: Download / Read the full report / Get the data

prompt_overrides.override_instruction: Open every caption with a specific statistic or percentage. Use 'Studies show...', 'Data reveals...', '[X]% of businesses...'. Back every claim with a reference to a data source or measurable outcome. Avoid fluffy language.

Open every video script with a shocking statistic as the hook. Example: 'Did you know [X]% of [audience] never [problem]? Here's the data that explains why.' Build the entire script around proving a data-backed thesis.

All generated posts must include at least one specific metric. Replace aspirational language with specific, measurable outcomes. Use charts, data visualisations, and case study references in visual directions.

example_hooks: 87% of social media posts get zero engagement. Here's what the 13% do differently.
Brands that post consistently 5x per week grow audiences 3x faster. The data:
We analysed 10,000 posts. The #1 predictor of virality isn't what you think.
Average conversion rate for this industry: 2.3%. Here's how to beat it.
Companies that invest in content marketing generate 3x more leads at 62% lower cost.

platform_notes.LinkedIn: Primary platform. Long-form data posts perform best. Include charts as attachments.
platform_notes.Twitter/X: Data threads perform well. Lead with the stat, break down in thread.
platform_notes.Instagram: Use infographic carousels. Data visualisation over text-heavy posts.
platform_notes.Facebook: Link to full reports or studies for click-through.
